reports.title: [Fixture] Sample research report — AI agent companies on social media
reports.tldr: Talk about AI agent companies on X and LinkedIn over March–April 2026 splits cleanly into two camps: **builders** sharing concrete shipping milestones (cost-per-task, eval scores, customer deployments) and **commentators** debating whether "agentic" is a category or a feature. Builders are gaining airtime: posts citing measurable outcomes get 3–4× more saves than thought-leadership threads, and the most-referenced launches are vertical agents (sales, support, ops) rather than horizontal "do anything" platforms. Skepticism is concentrated around evals — public eval suites are widely seen as overfit, and several practitioners are calling for outcome-anchored benchmarks rather than capability scores.
reports.body:
# AI agent companies on social media — March/April 2026

## TL;DR

Talk about AI agent companies on X and LinkedIn over March–April 2026 splits cleanly into two
camps: **builders** sharing concrete shipping milestones (cost-per-task, eval scores, customer
deployments) and **commentators** debating whether "agentic" is a category or a feature. Builders
are gaining airtime: posts citing measurable outcomes get 3–4× more saves than thought-leadership
threads, and the most-referenced launches are vertical agents (sales, support, ops) rather than
horizontal "do anything" platforms. Skepticism is concentrated around evals — public eval suites
are widely seen as overfit, and several practitioners are calling for outcome-anchored
benchmarks rather than capability scores.

## Scope

- Time window: 2026-03-01 to 2026-04-28
- Platforms: X (formerly Twitter), LinkedIn, Hacker News front-page discussions, selected
  Substack newsletters
- Search seeds: "AI agent", "agentic", "tool-use", "MCP", "agent eval", "vertical agent"
- Languages: English-only for v1

## Key themes

1. **Vertical beats horizontal — for now.** Sales SDR agents, customer-support triage, and
   ops-automation agents drove the highest-engagement launch posts. The "general agent" pitch
   appears to be in a trough.
2. **Cost-per-task is the new latency.** Posts that lead with `$ per resolved ticket` or
   `tokens per workflow` outperform those that lead with model size or context window.
3. **Eval fatigue.** Multiple practitioners (Hamel Husain, Eugene Yan, Chip Huyen) wrote that
   public agent benchmarks are saturated and increasingly meaningless — the camp is calling for
   customer-anchored "did the work get done" metrics instead.
4. **MCP as connective tissue.** Adoption discussion shifted from "what is MCP" in March to
   "which MCP servers are production-grade" in April. Shopify, Linear, and Stripe MCP servers
   were referenced as exemplars; the long tail is still characterized as fragile.
5. **Agent observability.** A small but growing thread: tools like Langfuse, Helicone, and
   Braintrust are being treated as table-stakes rather than nice-to-haves.

## Notable voices

- **Nathan Lambert** — pushed an "outcome benchmark" thesis in two long X threads; ~14k
  combined engagements.
- **Andrew Ng** — LinkedIn essay arguing agentic workflows beat single-shot prompting; broadly
  reshared by the enterprise crowd.
- **Eugene Yan** — repeatedly highlighted production failure modes (silent tool errors, missing
  retries) — drives a lot of the "evals don't capture this" conversation.
- **Swyx** — chronicled the MCP ecosystem week-over-week; functions as the de-facto changelog.
- **Aravind Srinivas (Perplexity)** — repositioned Perplexity messaging from "answer engine" to
  "research agent" mid-window.

## Conflicts and outliers

- A noisy minority argues "agents are just fancy if-statements" and that all current value is
  from improved tool-use prompting, not from any new abstraction. Most-quoted: François Chollet,
  several anonymous tech-Twitter accounts.
- Disagreement on memory: long-term memory (vector + summarization) vs. session-scoped state.
  Outcome-anchored builders trend toward session-scoped; "general agent" advocates push
  long-term memory hard.

## Gaps

- Limited Chinese-language coverage in this window — a follow-up should add Weibo / Zhihu /
  WeChat-public-account sources before drawing global conclusions.
- Pricing data is anecdotal. No public dataset of `$/resolved-task` exists; numbers cited are
  from individual launch posts and may be cherry-picked.
- Hard to disentangle paid promotion from organic conversation, especially on LinkedIn.

## Sources

- [Hamel Husain — eval rant](https://x.com/hamelhusain/status/1771234567890)
- [Andrew Ng — agentic workflows essay](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/agentic-workflows-andrew-ng/)
- [Eugene Yan — production failure modes](https://eugeneyan.com/writing/agent-failures/)
- [swyx — MCP weekly digest](https://www.latent.space/p/mcp-weekly-april-2026)
- [Hacker News — "Show HN: Production-grade MCP servers"](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=39876543)

## Analyst commentary

The biggest near-term opportunity is **outcome-anchored evaluation tooling** — the gap between
"my agent passed AgentBench" and "my agent saved my customer 6 hours/week" is the loudest
unmet need surfaced this window. Vendors that ship a credible "did the work" measurement
substrate will likely capture both the eval-fatigue contingent and the procurement teams now
asking for proof points beyond demos.
